new_jobs.title: Director of Development, College of Medicine
new_jobs.description:
Department

FSU Division of University Advancement

Florida State University is currently in the leadership phase of a comprehensive campaign. As the leadership phase of the campaign begins, the Division of University Advancement is accelerating the hiring of additional development officers’ campus-wide and is excited to build out its team. For Advancement professionals who are interested in being a part of a comprehensive campaign from the beginning for their career development, the timing is ideal to join our team.

Responsibilities

The Director of Development will strategically execute a personal solicitation program resulting in philanthropic support by maximizing the identification, cultivation, solicitation and stewardship of prospects for gifts at the $100,000 and up level for College of Medicine. This individual will secure, on an annual basis, private support at the major and principal gift level and will be responsible for identification of potential major and principal gift prospects. The Director of Development will be expected to uphold and exemplify the operating practices of the Division of University Advancement while raising philanthropic dollars in support of the mission of Florida State University.

Works with the Dean, and the other members of the College of Medicine development, communication and alumni team to develop and execute strategic fundraising initiatives on behalf of the College and the university. Develops, coordinates, and executes an annual major gift plan, implementing fund-raising activities designed to meet an established monetary goal while also hitting a set suite of metrics. Establishes new relationships resulting in philanthropic investment, maintains existing relationships, which may generate new philanthropic investments, and provides prospect/donor-related assessments and feedback for the College and the Division of University Advancement. Identifies, qualifies and sustains a donor prospect portfolio with individuals who have a giving capacity of $500,000 or more. Travels frequently, and secures gifts and pledges from alumni, parents, faculty, staff, and friends of FSU.

Exhibits and promotes a comprehensive understanding of the philanthropic priorities of the College, effectively matching the specific interests of prospects to those needs. Plans for and initiates direct activities with prospects that are designed to enhance the affiliation between the prospect and FSU. Effectively communicates FSU’s mission and represents FSU’s culture to external stakeholders.

Central Development Collaboration

Coordinates with the Division of University Advancement colleagues to identify prospects for planned giving, corporate and foundation giving in partnership with the Office of University Research, and principal giving in partnership with the Principal Gifts Team. Protects confidential information. Perform other related duties as required and assigned.

Administrative/Events

Assists in the facilitation of meetings, communications, informational exchanges and project / philanthropic coordination with the faculty and faculty chairs. Prepares proposals and gift agreements as required for proper gift documentation. Prepares correspondence with prospects and documents communications, solicitation activity and gift information in compliance with the FSU Foundation’s reporting guidelines. Creates, inspires, and maintains donor loyalty, interest and enthusiasm through stewardship communication and activities designed to express recognition, appreciation and the impact of their gift(s). Implementation of such a stewardship plan may include but should not be limited to letters, communication of unit or university initiatives, invitations to appropriate activities, meetings, events, student engagement or the facilitation of campus visits.

Board/Volunteer Management

Assists in the management of all volunteer boards associated with the College of Medicine.

Qualifications

Bachelor's degree and six years of experience; or a high school diploma/equivalent and ten years of experience. (Note: a combination of post high school education and experience equal to ten years.)

Must have a valid Florida or Georgia Driver's License, or the ability to obtain prior to hire.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in Higher Education, University Comprehensive Capital Campaigns, Academic Medicine or Healthcare Fundraising.

University Information

One of the nation's elite research universities, Florida State University preserves, expands, and disseminates knowledge in the sciences, technology, arts, humanities, and professions, while embracing a philosophy of learning strongly rooted in the traditions of the liberal arts and critical thinking. Founded in 1851, Florida State University is the oldest continuous site of higher education in Florida. FSU is a community steeped in tradition that fosters research and encourages creativity. At FSU, there’s the excitement of being part of a vibrant academic and professional community, surrounded by people whose ideas are shaping tomorrow’s news!

Learn more about Florida State University and our campuses.

Use our FSU Total Compensation Calculator to calculate Total Compensation options based on potential salary, benefits and retirement contributions, earned leave, and other employment-related perks.

Applicants are required to complete the online application with all applicable information. Applications must include all work history up to ten years, and education details even if attaching a resume.

They are naturally organized, detail-oriented, and solutions-focused, with the ability to translate complexity into clear processes, measurable outcomes, and reliable execution.

Operational Leadership & Team Development

Lead, mentor, and develop a diverse operational organization encompassing Actionable Imagery, Partner Relations, and VDC/BIM functions.
Foster a culture of trust, accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
Build effective working relationships across diverse personalities, experience levels, and functional disciplines.

Support hiring, onboarding, and development of operational team members and future operational leaders.

Organizational Stabilization & Operational Scaling

Evaluate team structures and recommend organizational improvements that support future growth.
Create operational clarity through defined ownership, reporting structures, escalation paths, and performance expectations.
Establish scalable management systems capable of supporting future operational expansion.
Drive organizational alignment across operational functions while maintaining service quality and delivery consistency.

Cross-Functional Alignment & Delivery Excellence

new_jobs.title: Branch Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
Join the YMCA of Metro Atlanta Association as the Branch Executive Director for the Villages at Carver YMCA!

Job Description

The YMCA of Metro Atlanta is seeking a passionate and forward-thinking executive director with a proven, successful track record of developing a strong branch board, increasing annual giving, engaging community through increasing membership and programs while advancing the Y’s impact in the community.

This highly motivated leader who is impact-focused and entrepreneurial by nature will report directly to the Group Vice President and will have primary responsibility for day-to-day operations. Experience in and a demonstrated track record of the areas of staff supervision, membership and program development, fiscal management, fundraising, volunteer cultivation, and community engagement are required. The continued development and coaching of an experienced staff team to new levels of service will be key to achieving full potential.

Qualifications

REQUIREMENTS:

Bachelor's Degree and 8+ years overall leadership experience or high school diploma and 12+ years of directly relevant experience with the Y or closely related non-profit
2+ years of successful experience managing large teams in an executive level/senior management position
Business management skills and demonstrated ability to lead people
Proven ability to establish and maintain collaborations with community organization, corporations, and government agencies
Strong attention to detail
Passionate commitment to the YMCA mission, values, and purpose
CPR and First Aid certifications required within 30 days of hire

Preferred Requirements

Bachelor's degree in human services, social services, business or equivalent
Experience in directing total operations through volunteer development, supervision of staff, development and monitoring of branch budget, marketing and public relations, and program development
Hands-on experience working with a diverse population in a YMCA strongly preferred
12+ years of directly relevant experience with the YMCA or closely related non-profit
4+ years of management experience, strategic vision and relationship building preferably in a YMCA or other nonprofit agency

Essential Functions

Establish a culture of quality and service to meet and exceed the goals and objectives set forth in the strategic plan and standards established by the Board of Directors.
Represents and promotes the Y in the local community by developing strategic, positive working and collaborative relationships with other organizations, businesses, and governmental entities.
Develop, manage, and monitor a balanced operating budget to meet or exceed budgeted targets.
Develop and direct high-quality, relationship-based member engagement strategies.
Provide metrics to measure program/membership impact, staff, and program and membership satisfaction.
Maintain branch and association certifications in CPR, Multimedia First Aid, Blood-borne Pathogens, Metro Atlanta YMCA new staff orientation, branch orientation and branch emergency procedures. Manage delivery of related programs to exceed YMCA standards and program objectives.
Participate in branch and Association-wide activities and initiatives.
Act as the LEAD campaigner for Why It Matters Annual Giving Campaign including but not limited to volunteer recruitment, training and support, and lead as an integral member of the management team committed to reaching stated fund-raising goals.
Serve on the Executive Cabinet, liaison with appropriate professional groups in the community and serve on Metro Task Forces.
Effective board development including recruitment of board members who represent the branch community and developing a strong board committee structure.
Ensures revenue growth through a climate of innovation to develop member-focused programs that support the Y mission, goals, and strategies.
Effectively mentor, supervise, and develop branch staff and volunteers and build an exceptional culture to motivate them to achieve their personal and professional goals.
Responsible for facility management and maintenance to ensure high-quality facilities, grounds, and equipment. A culture of risk management and safety is maintained at the branch. Maintains required safety and training certifications, Emergency Action Plans, SDS Manuals, etc.
